key_points:
### Key Compliance Points — Credit Information Reporting (Amendment) Directions, 2025
*(RBI/DOR/2025-26/116, dated 2025-12-04; effective 2026-07-01)*

- **What it does:** Moves credit-information reporting from monthly to a **near-real-time, four-times-a-month** cycle, by substituting **para 10(2)** and adding/replacing paras 12(10) and 14 (RBI/DOR/2025-26/116, 2025-12-04).
- **Para 10(2) (substituted) — reporting cadence:**
  - Submit credit information as on the **9th, 16th, 23rd and last day** of each month (the **"reference dates"**).
  - **Full file** (all active accounts + accounts closed since last reference date) as on month-end → to CICs **by the 5th** of the next month.
  - For the 9th/16th/23rd → submit only **"incremental accounts"** (newly opened, closed, changed-since-last-date, or overdue accounts; a change in days-past-due also counts) **within 4 calendar days** of each reference date.
  - CICs report defaulting CIs on the **DAKSH portal** to RBI half-yearly (31 Mar / 30 Sep).
- **Para 12(10) (inserted):** a CI shall report the **CKYC number** of borrowers to CICs wherever available.
- **Para 14 (substituted):** rejected data must be **rectified and re-submitted** before/along with the next reference-date submission.
- **Commencement:** **July 1, 2026** (illustrative timelines in Annex-VI).

_Verify with the compliance team before acting._
what_changed:
### What Changed
*(Amendment: RBI/DOR/2025-26/116, dated 2025-12-04 · effective 2026-07-01)*

| Provision | Change type | Effect |
|---|---|---|
| **Para 10(2)** | **Substituted** | Reporting on 4 reference dates/month (9th, 16th, 23rd, last day); full file by 5th of next month; incremental accounts within 4 days; DAKSH-portal monitoring of non-compliant CIs. |
| **Para 12(10)** | **Inserted** | Report borrower **CKYC number** to CICs where available. |
| **Para 14** | **Substituted** | Rectify and re-submit rejected data before/with the next reference-date submission. |

- **Effective date:** July 1, 2026.

_Verify with the compliance team before acting._
implications:
**Why it matters for NaBFID:** NaBFID's credit-reporting operations must shift from a monthly batch to a **weekly incremental** rhythm, with tight 4-day turnarounds. This needs systems and process changes well before July 1, 2026, or NaBFID risks appearing on the DAKSH non-compliance list.

*Illustrative example:* When a borrower repays or an account turns overdue on the 15th, that change must be captured and reported as an "incremental account" by the 20th (within 4 days of the 16th reference date) — not held for a month-end batch.

_Verify with the compliance team before acting._
